en: We thought for our first show of the year, it might be worth pulling back a bit and talking not just about Minneapolis,
ko: 올해 첫 방송으로 미니애폴리스나 ICE만이 아니라 그것이 속한 거대한 기관인 국토안보부에 대해

en: or even just ICE, but about the massive agency it's a part of, the Department of Homeland Security.
ko: 좀 물러서서 이야기하는 게 좋겠다고 생각했어.

en: Because as you're about to see, a discussion about it, its funding, and the sweeping powers that we've given it,
ko: 보게 되겠지만 DHS와 그 예산, 우리가 부여한 광범위한 권한에 대한 논의는 오래전에 했어야 했어.

en: is long overdue. So tonight, let's look at DHS.
And let's start with its origins, because while you might assume DHS is a long-standing part of American government,
ko: 오늘 밤 DHS를 살펴보자. 먼저 기원부터. DHS가 미국 정부의 오래된 기관이라고 생각할 수 있지만

en: it's just 23 years old. It was founded in response to 9-11.
ko: 겨우 23년 됐어. 9/11 대응으로 만들어졌거든. 9/11을 기억하기엔 너무 어린 사람들을 위해

en: And if you are too young to remember 9-11, and I will pause long enough for the rest of us
ko: 나머지 사람들이 그 문장의 정신적 충격을 느낄 만큼 잠시 멈추겠어.

en: to experience the psychic gut punch of that sentence.
ko: 나머지 사람들이 그 문장의 정신적 충격을 느낄 만큼 잠시 멈추겠어.

en: Very basically, in the immediate aftermath of the most devastating terrorist attack in its history,
ko: 아주 간단히 말하면 역사상 가장 파괴적인 테러 공격 직후 미국은 비명을 지르기 시작했고

en: America started screaming and didn't really stop for a decade.
And while thankfully, the Riyadh Comedy Festival has since healed the world through comedy,
ko: 거의 10년간 멈추지 않았어. 다행히 리야드 코미디 페스티벌이 코미디로 세상을 치유했고

en: and we don't need to be worried about who or what was actually responsible for 9-11.
ko: 9/11의 실제 책임자가 누구인지 걱정할 필요가 없지만 당시 주요 우려는

en: At the time, a major concern was that prior to the attacks, there'd be a lack of communication
ko: 공격 전에 연방 기관 간 소통과 정보 공유가 부족했다는 거였어.

en: and information sharing between federal agencies.
ko: 공격 전에 연방 기관 간 소통과 정보 공유가 부족했다는 거였어.

en: So, the Bush administration agreed to combine a bunch of them under a single banner
ko: 그래서 부시 행정부는 여러 기관을 하나의 깃발 아래 통합해 조율을 개선하기로 했어. 하지만 그때도

en: to improve coordination. But even back then, some felt that that was a bad idea,
to the point that it was openly discussed in news coverage.
ko: 나쁜 아이디어라고 느끼는 사람들이 있었고 뉴스에서 공개적으로 논의됐어.

en: What do Secret Service agents have in common with animal disease researchers?
Nothing. Except that in three months, both will be working for the new Homeland Security Department.
An organizational chart they could give a management expert nightmares.
ko: 경영 전문가에게 악몽을 줄 조직도. 22개 기관, 17만 명의 직원. 정부에서 3번째로 큰 부서.

en: Twenty-two agencies, 170,000 workers.
The third-largest department in the government. Okay, first, it is a little weird to introduce
a government agency like it's a new sitcom. What happens when an uptight TSA agent
ko: 하지만 더 큰 요점인 이게 급하게 짜깁기됐다는 건 맞아. 갑자기 DHS에는

en: and a laid-back FEMA official have to move in together?
Find out in Homeland Security Thursdays on TBS.
ko: 비밀경호국부터 해안경비대, FEMA, TSA까지 다 들어갔지만

en: But the larger point there, that this thing had been hastily cobbled together, very much holds.
ko: 핵심 대테러 기관들은 빠졌어. DHS는 FBI, CIA, 국방부의 수사,

en: Because all of a sudden, DHS contained everything from the Secret Service, to the Coast Guard, to FEMA and TSA.
ko: 정보, 군사력 없이 운영해야 했거든. 근데 걱정 마. 돼지 연구원들은 있으니까.

en: But for everything that went in, some key counter-terrorism agencies were left out.
Because DHS had to operate without the investigative, intelligence and military powers of the FBI, CIA
and the Pentagon. But don't worry, because remember, they had those pig researchers, so they were pretty much set.
